title: Run round-trip and instantiation tests
description: Instantiate the module and exercise boundary calls to verify marshalling, memory safety, and import resolution.
body: Use when validating a WASM build or interop change. Procedure: instantiate the module in the target runtime with the real import object and confirm no missing-import error; run round-trip tests that pass strings/arrays/structs across the boundary and assert identical values back; repeat buffer round-trips while watching linear-memory size for leaks; trigger a memory.grow and confirm views read correctly after refreshing. Report which crossings round-tripped, whether memory stayed bounded, and any instantiation failure. This skill runs local/test-runtime checks only and must not deploy to production. Validation signals include round-trip equality, bounded memory, and successful instantiation. Risk boundary: if a round-trip corrupts data or memory grows without bound, treat it as a blocking defect rather than adjusting the assertion.
